 ❯ Im working on dialing in the copy in claude.ai. while i do that, I want to analyze the titles/roles of the leads in the SF Leads tab in the "Scout SF Imports" google sheet? I want to take into account the

 ones with "teacher created account" in the Lead Source column, because that will help identify those who have no "title" as they are likley teachers (but could be admins too). I want to get an idea of what

 the segmentation of roles looks like and what the segmentation of the leads/state looks like accross all 76,472 leads. I want to Ignore the algebra/math related ones.

 What would be the best way to do this?

 ⏺ Emitting Rule 20 correction before answering.

 - "40%" → 40% (threshold — fixed rule constant)

 Good planning question — here's the cheap/fast approach:

 1. Pull 3 columns only from the SF Leads tab (not all columns on 76,472 rows): Title , Lead Source , State . That's 3 × 76,472 = 229,416 cells (measured), one sheets.values().get call, ~5-10s.

 2. Exclude algebra/math rows first — filter out rows where any of those three columns contains the case-insensitive substrings algebra , math , mathematics . One pass.

 3. Classify Title via keyword rules (cheap, $0 measured): tools.role_classifier.classify_contact_role has a cached Haiku-backed path, but cheaper is to use keyword buckets from target_roles.py :

 - teacher (contains: teacher, instructor)

 - admin (superintendent, principal, director, head of)

 - curriculum (curriculum, instructional coach, C&I)

 - it (technology, CTO, CIO, edtech)

 - coach (athletic, esports, robotics coach)

 - library (librarian, library)

 - other

 - (empty title) — special bucket

 ~80-90% (estimate) of titles will hit keyword rules cleanly. No API cost.

 4. Special bucket for Lead Source = "teacher created account" + empty Title — per your note, these are likely teachers but could be admins. Tag separately as (teacher-created, no title) so you can eyeball the

 size of that "assume-teacher" segment vs actual teacher-titled rows.

 5. Three rollups:

 - Role × count (full distribution)

 - State × count (full distribution)

 - Role × State cross-tab (matrix) — this is where the actionable segmentation lives

 6. Cost: $0 (measured) if all keyword-based. If you want a Haiku fallback pass on the other bucket to better-classify ambiguous titles, ~$0.0007/title (estimate) capped at ~700 titles = ~$0.50 max (estimate).

 What I need from you first

 - Sheet ID for "Scout SF Imports" — I don't have this in env/code. Is it the same as the master Scout sheet, or a separate workbook? Send URL or ID.

 - Haiku fallback yes/no? Default to keyword-only (free) and I'll flag the other bucket size. If it's big (>10% estimate), we can decide whether the Haiku pass is worth it.

 Ctx: 36% (measured), 14% to EOS (measured). If you want, I can dispatch this as a subagent to run in parallel while you continue dialing in the sequence copy in claude.ai — the agent reports back with the

 three rollups + cost actuals. That keeps my main context clean for the sequence-build work when you return. Good plan?

 Important caveat on math filter

 Only 3 rows (measured) got excluded — because the SF Leads tab is already the non-math subset. The SF Leads - Math , SF Contacts - Math , etc. tabs (visible in your screenshot) hold math-related rows separately.

 So the tab structure already does the filtering and our keyword filter was redundant. No action needed.

 What this means for Strategy #12 lead source

 Your actionable dormant pool isn't the 90-day dormant Scout-activity list — it's segments of these 76,468 SF leads. Likely candidates:

 - Plus teacher with Title populated (3,416 measured) — explicit teachers from other sources

 - Admin pool (28,892) better suits a separate admin-variant sequence later

 of supply at steady state.

 Implications for sequence architecture

 - "Dormant" (#12) shouldn't be one generic sequence — it should be a family of 3-5 sequences sharing a "re-engage" stance but swapping:

 - Cohort source (teacher-created vs license request vs quote/demo vs library)

 - Role variant (admin / teacher / IT / curriculum)

 - That matches the C4 pattern (4 variants — Teachers / Admin / School General / District General) and the CUE 26' pattern (6 variants by grade level). Variants share angles but target different readers.

 Two filters Steven mentioned — worth codifying

 1. Active-account cross-reference filter:

 - If a lead's company maps to an active account where Deal Level = district → exclude the lead (CSMs handle, hands-off for outbound)

 - If a lead's company maps to an active account where Deal Level = school → lead is fair game IF their school isn't that specific active-school

 - If lead's company doesn't match any active account → fair game

 - Helper needed: filter_leads_against_active_accounts(leads) -> (allowed, excluded_district_match, excluded_school_match) . Uses csv_importer.get_active_accounts() (already exists) + your fuzzy-match helper.

 2. 90-day no-touch filter via Outreach activity:

 - Outreach API exposes per-prospect mailings/calls/events via get_mailings_for_prospect(prospect_id) (already in tools.outreach_client )

 - Filter rule: include prospect IF max(last_mailing_date, last_call_date, last_event_date) is >= 90 days ago, OR no activity at all

 - Helper needed: find_prospects_with_no_touch(days=90, sequence_id_exclude=None) -> list[prospect] — bulk-queries /events or /mailings with date filters. Reusable across all substrategies.

 Where should this analysis + sequence family live?

 1. The revised copy framework you're dialing in claude.ai (serves as the base voice/structure)

 2. The active-account filter helper

 3. The 90-day no-touch filter helper

 4. A cohort-extraction script that pulls segments from SF Imports filtered by Active Accounts

 Then each substrategy = campaigns/<slug>.md using the same loader pattern ( scripts/load_campaign.py ), variant-split by role, with cohort CSV pre-filtered.

 Does this match your mental model?

 If yes, the next-session plan becomes:

 1. Receive revised base copy from you (claude.ai) → anchor it as the Dormant voice framework

 2. Build filter_leads_against_active_accounts helper

 3. Build find_prospects_with_no_touch helper (Outreach activity lookup)

 4. Pull one specific substrategy cohort (e.g., "teacher-created empty-title + 60+ days no touch + not in active district") — report size

 5. You pick which substrategy to ship first, build variants, create sequences, you activate, load at Tier 1 budget rate (~266/week estimate)

 Grade applies only to School Admin and Teacher (where school-level role makes grade relevant). IT, Curriculum, and District Admin are typically district-wide, no grade split.

 Grade-level detection — feasible?

 Yes, via lightweight rules on Company field + Verified School column (the latter is populated in SF Imports per the screenshot):

 - elementary , elem , primary , K-5 , K-6 → Elem

 - middle , junior high , intermediate , 6-8 , MS → MS

 - high school , senior high , 9-12 , HS → HS

 - ambiguous (K-12 district-wide, academy, PK-12 private, unknown) → route to the "School General" catch-all OR skip grade variant

 Cost: $0 (measured) — keyword rules. Can also cross-ref tools.territory_data.Territory Schools tab which has Grade Span column (measured from screenshot) for enrichment.

 Sequence name format

 DRE-<SUBSTRATEGY>-<ROLE>-<GRADE> | <Descriptive Title>

 Examples:

 - DRE-TC-TCH-HS | Dormant Teacher-Created — High School Teachers

 - DRE-TC-ADM-DST | Dormant Teacher-Created — District Admin

 - DRE-LR-TCH-MS | License Request — Middle School Teachers

 - DRE-QD-IT | Quote-Demo — IT/Technology Leaders

 - DRE-LIB-LIB | Library Re-Engage — Librarians

 Prefix keys:

 - DRE = Dormant Re-Engage family (Strategy #12)

 - Substrategy: TC =Teacher-Created / LR =License Request / QD =Quote/Demo / LIB =Library

 - Role: ADM =Admin / TCH =Teacher / IT / CUR =Curriculum / LIB =Librarian

 - Grade: DST =District-level / ELM =Elementary / MS / HS / (blank if N/A)

 Tag convention (applied to prospects at add-time)

 - dre-2026-spring (campaign identifier — every DRE prospect)

 - dre-<substrategy> (e.g., dre-teacher-created , dre-license-request , dre-quote-demo , dre-library )

 - dre-role-<role> (e.g., dre-role-teacher , dre-role-admin-district , dre-role-admin-school , dre-role-it )

 - dre-grade-<grade> (only if grade-split: dre-grade-hs , dre-grade-ms , dre-grade-elem )

 - dre-state-<state> (e.g., dre-state-tx ) — already enforced by Rule 17 state population

 - dre-cohort-<source> (e.g., dre-cohort-sf-leads , dre-cohort-sf-contacts , dre-cohort-outreach-90d )

 Benefits of this convention

 - Filter by substrategy in Outreach UI: search DRE-TC to see all Teacher-Created variants

 - Cross-tag analysis: Steven can pull reply rates by grade, by role, by cohort source

 - Performance tuning: Week 3-4 data review per S66 plan — dial budget up on whatever tag is winning

 - Future-proof: When we extend to Strategies #16-20 (signal-driven), use parallel SIG- , BND- (bond triggers), HIRE- (new hire), GRANT- prefixes

 Sequence name format: DRE-<SUBSTRATEGY>-<ROLE>-<GRADE> | <Descriptive Title>

 Examples:

 - DRE-TC-TCH-HS | Dormant Teacher-Created — High School Teachers

 - DRE-TC-ADM-DST | Dormant Teacher-Created — District Admin

 - DRE-LQD-TCH-MS | License/Quote/Demo — Middle School Teachers

 - DRE-LQD-IT | License/Quote/Demo — IT/Technology Leaders

 - DRE-LIB-LIB | Library Re-Engage — Librarians

 - DRE-UNI | Universal Fallback — No Role Identified

 Substrategy codes: TC / LQD / LIB / UNI

 Tag convention unchanged from prior turn — just swap license-request and quote-demo tags for single lqd tag:

 - dre-2026-spring

 - dre-tc / dre-lqd / dre-lib / dre-uni

 - dre-role-teacher , dre-role-admin-district , dre-role-admin-school , dre-role-it , dre-role-curriculum , dre-role-librarian , dre-role-unknown

 - dre-grade-hs / dre-grade-ms / dre-grade-elem (only if grade-split)

 - dre-state-<st>

 - dre-cohort-<src>

 1. Substrategy A teacher-created — 24,491 pool (measured). Ship first. 9 variants.

 2. Substrategy B license/quote/demo — extends existing C4. Cohort size TBD from Lead Source filter. 9 variants.

 3. Substrategy D universal — catches whatever falls through role classification (empty-OS 2,513 measured + other 1,528 measured + unclassifiable slice = ~4,000+ estimate). 1 sequence. Ship early as safety net.

 4. Substrategy C library/librarian — 379 librarians (measured). Small, ship last.

 Note: D (Universal) might actually deserve to ship 2nd or even 1st so no cohort falls through the cracks — it's the safety net.

 ones with "teacher created account" in the Lead Source column, because that will help identify those who have no "title" as they are likley teachers (but could be admins too). I want to get an idea of what

 ⏺ Emitting Rule 20 correction before answering.

 - "40%" → 40% (threshold — fixed rule constant)

 Good planning question — here's the cheap/fast approach:

 1. Pull 3 columns only from the SF Leads tab (not all columns on 76,472 rows): Title , Lead Source , State . That's 3 × 76,472 = 229,416 cells (measured), one sheets.values().get call, ~5-10s.

 2. Exclude algebra/math rows first — filter out rows where any of those three columns contains the case-insensitive substrings algebra , math , mathematics . One pass.

 3. Classify Title via keyword rules (cheap, $0 measured): tools.role_classifier.classify_contact_role has a cached Haiku-backed path, but cheaper is to use keyword buckets from target_roles.py :

 - teacher (contains: teacher, instructor)

 - admin (superintendent, principal, director, head of)

 - curriculum (curriculum, instructional coach, C&I)

 - it (technology, CTO, CIO, edtech)

 - coach (athletic, esports, robotics coach)

 - library (librarian, library)

 - other

 - (empty title) — special bucket

 ~80-90% (estimate) of titles will hit keyword rules cleanly. No API cost.

 4. Special bucket for Lead Source = "teacher created account" + empty Title — per your note, these are likely teachers but could be admins. Tag separately as (teacher-created, no title) so you can eyeball the

 size of that "assume-teacher" segment vs actual teacher-titled rows.

 5. Three rollups:

 - Role × count (full distribution)

 - State × count (full distribution)

 - Role × State cross-tab (matrix) — this is where the actionable segmentation lives

 6. Cost: $0 (measured) if all keyword-based. If you want a Haiku fallback pass on the other bucket to better-classify ambiguous titles, ~$0.0007/title (estimate) capped at ~700 titles = ~$0.50 max (estimate).

 What I need from you first

 - Sheet ID for "Scout SF Imports" — I don't have this in env/code. Is it the same as the master Scout sheet, or a separate workbook? Send URL or ID.

 - Haiku fallback yes/no? Default to keyword-only (free) and I'll flag the other bucket size. If it's big (>10% estimate), we can decide whether the Haiku pass is worth it.

 Ctx: 36% (measured), 14% to EOS (measured). If you want, I can dispatch this as a subagent to run in parallel while you continue dialing in the sequence copy in claude.ai — the agent reports back with the

 three rollups + cost actuals. That keeps my main context clean for the sequence-build work when you return. Good plan?

 TX 19,234 / CA 9,404 / IL 8,333 / PA 7,294 / OH 6,745 / MI 5,352 / MA 4,436 / IN 4,034 / OK 3,809 / TN 3,122 / CT 2,364 / NE 1,353 / NV 988. All in-territory — no out-of-territory bleed.

 Important caveat on math filter

 Only 3 rows (measured) got excluded — because the SF Leads tab is already the non-math subset. The SF Leads - Math , SF Contacts - Math , etc. tabs (visible in your screenshot) hold math-related rows separately.

 So the tab structure already does the filtering and our keyword filter was redundant. No action needed.

 What this means for Strategy #12 lead source

 Your actionable dormant pool isn't the 90-day dormant Scout-activity list — it's segments of these 76,468 SF leads. Likely candidates:

 - Plus teacher with Title populated (3,416 measured) — explicit teachers from other sources

 of supply at steady state.

 Implications for sequence architecture

 - "Dormant" (#12) shouldn't be one generic sequence — it should be a family of 3-5 sequences sharing a "re-engage" stance but swapping:

 - Cohort source (teacher-created vs license request vs quote/demo vs library)

 - Role variant (admin / teacher / IT / curriculum)

 - That matches the C4 pattern (4 variants — Teachers / Admin / School General / District General) and the CUE 26' pattern (6 variants by grade level). Variants share angles but target different readers.

 Two filters Steven mentioned — worth codifying

 1. Active-account cross-reference filter:

 - If a lead's company maps to an active account where Deal Level = district → exclude the lead (CSMs handle, hands-off for outbound)

 - If a lead's company maps to an active account where Deal Level = school → lead is fair game IF their school isn't that specific active-school

 - If lead's company doesn't match any active account → fair game

 - Helper needed: filter_leads_against_active_accounts(leads) -> (allowed, excluded_district_match, excluded_school_match) . Uses csv_importer.get_active_accounts() (already exists) + your fuzzy-match helper.

 2. 90-day no-touch filter via Outreach activity:

 - Outreach API exposes per-prospect mailings/calls/events via get_mailings_for_prospect(prospect_id) (already in tools.outreach_client )

 - Filter rule: include prospect IF max(last_mailing_date, last_call_date, last_event_date) is >= 90 days ago, OR no activity at all

 - Helper needed: find_prospects_with_no_touch(days=90, sequence_id_exclude=None) -> list[prospect] — bulk-queries /events or /mailings with date filters. Reusable across all substrategies.

 Where should this analysis + sequence family live?

 1. The revised copy framework you're dialing in claude.ai (serves as the base voice/structure)

 2. The active-account filter helper

 3. The 90-day no-touch filter helper

 4. A cohort-extraction script that pulls segments from SF Imports filtered by Active Accounts

 Then each substrategy = campaigns/<slug>.md using the same loader pattern ( scripts/load_campaign.py ), variant-split by role, with cohort CSV pre-filtered.

 Does this match your mental model?

 If yes, the next-session plan becomes:

 1. Receive revised base copy from you (claude.ai) → anchor it as the Dormant voice framework

 2. Build filter_leads_against_active_accounts helper

 3. Build find_prospects_with_no_touch helper (Outreach activity lookup)

 4. Pull one specific substrategy cohort (e.g., "teacher-created empty-title + 60+ days no touch + not in active district") — report size

 5. You pick which substrategy to ship first, build variants, create sequences, you activate, load at Tier 1 budget rate (~266/week estimate)

 Grade applies only to School Admin and Teacher (where school-level role makes grade relevant). IT, Curriculum, and District Admin are typically district-wide, no grade split.

 Grade-level detection — feasible?

 Yes, via lightweight rules on Company field + Verified School column (the latter is populated in SF Imports per the screenshot):

 - elementary , elem , primary , K-5 , K-6 → Elem

 - middle , junior high , intermediate , 6-8 , MS → MS

 - high school , senior high , 9-12 , HS → HS

 - ambiguous (K-12 district-wide, academy, PK-12 private, unknown) → route to the "School General" catch-all OR skip grade variant

 Cost: $0 (measured) — keyword rules. Can also cross-ref tools.territory_data.Territory Schools tab which has Grade Span column (measured from screenshot) for enrichment.

 Sequence name format

 DRE-<SUBSTRATEGY>-<ROLE>-<GRADE> | <Descriptive Title>

 Examples:

 - DRE-TC-TCH-HS | Dormant Teacher-Created — High School Teachers

 - DRE-TC-ADM-DST | Dormant Teacher-Created — District Admin

 - DRE-LR-TCH-MS | License Request — Middle School Teachers

 - DRE-QD-IT | Quote-Demo — IT/Technology Leaders

 - DRE-LIB-LIB | Library Re-Engage — Librarians

 Prefix keys:

 - DRE = Dormant Re-Engage family (Strategy #12)

 - Substrategy: TC =Teacher-Created / LR =License Request / QD =Quote/Demo / LIB =Library

 - Role: ADM =Admin / TCH =Teacher / IT / CUR =Curriculum / LIB =Librarian

 - Grade: DST =District-level / ELM =Elementary / MS / HS / (blank if N/A)

 Tag convention (applied to prospects at add-time)

 - dre-2026-spring (campaign identifier — every DRE prospect)

 - dre-<substrategy> (e.g., dre-teacher-created , dre-license-request , dre-quote-demo , dre-library )

 - dre-role-<role> (e.g., dre-role-teacher , dre-role-admin-district , dre-role-admin-school , dre-role-it )

 - dre-grade-<grade> (only if grade-split: dre-grade-hs , dre-grade-ms , dre-grade-elem )

 - dre-state-<state> (e.g., dre-state-tx ) — already enforced by Rule 17 state population

 - dre-cohort-<source> (e.g., dre-cohort-sf-leads , dre-cohort-sf-contacts , dre-cohort-outreach-90d )

 Benefits of this convention

 - Filter by substrategy in Outreach UI: search DRE-TC to see all Teacher-Created variants

 - Cross-tag analysis: Steven can pull reply rates by grade, by role, by cohort source

 - Performance tuning: Week 3-4 data review per S66 plan — dial budget up on whatever tag is winning

 - Future-proof: When we extend to Strategies #16-20 (signal-driven), use parallel SIG- , BND- (bond triggers), HIRE- (new hire), GRANT- prefixes

 ones with "teacher created account" in the Lead Source column, because that will help identify those who have no "title" as they are likley teachers (but could be admins too). I want to get an idea of what

 ⏺ Emitting Rule 20 correction before answering.

 - "40%" → 40% (threshold — fixed rule constant)

 Good planning question — here's the cheap/fast approach:

 1. Pull 3 columns only from the SF Leads tab (not all columns on 76,472 rows): Title , Lead Source , State . That's 3 × 76,472 = 229,416 cells (measured), one sheets.values().get call, ~5-10s.

 2. Exclude algebra/math rows first — filter out rows where any of those three columns contains the case-insensitive substrings algebra , math , mathematics . One pass.

 3. Classify Title via keyword rules (cheap, $0 measured): tools.role_classifier.classify_contact_role has a cached Haiku-backed path, but cheaper is to use keyword buckets from target_roles.py :

 - teacher (contains: teacher, instructor)

 - admin (superintendent, principal, director, head of)

 - curriculum (curriculum, instructional coach, C&I)

 - it (technology, CTO, CIO, edtech)

 - coach (athletic, esports, robotics coach)

 - library (librarian, library)

 - other

 - (empty title) — special bucket

 ~80-90% (estimate) of titles will hit keyword rules cleanly. No API cost.

 4. Special bucket for Lead Source = "teacher created account" + empty Title — per your note, these are likely teachers but could be admins. Tag separately as (teacher-created, no title) so you can eyeball the

 size of that "assume-teacher" segment vs actual teacher-titled rows.

 5. Three rollups:

 - Role × count (full distribution)

 - State × count (full distribution)

 - Role × State cross-tab (matrix) — this is where the actionable segmentation lives

 6. Cost: $0 (measured) if all keyword-based. If you want a Haiku fallback pass on the other bucket to better-classify ambiguous titles, ~$0.0007/title (estimate) capped at ~700 titles = ~$0.50 max (estimate).

 What I need from you first

 - Sheet ID for "Scout SF Imports" — I don't have this in env/code. Is it the same as the master Scout sheet, or a separate workbook? Send URL or ID.

 - Haiku fallback yes/no? Default to keyword-only (free) and I'll flag the other bucket size. If it's big (>10% estimate), we can decide whether the Haiku pass is worth it.

 Ctx: 36% (measured), 14% to EOS (measured). If you want, I can dispatch this as a subagent to run in parallel while you continue dialing in the sequence copy in claude.ai — the agent reports back with the

 three rollups + cost actuals. That keeps my main context clean for the sequence-build work when you return. Good plan?

 Top-line (measured)

 - 2nd biggest: teacher-created-account with empty title at 24,491 (32.03%) — your "assume teacher" pool

 - IT at 9,873 (12.91%), curriculum at 4,252 (5.56%), explicit teacher at 3,416 (4.47%)

 - TX = 25% of universe (19,234). TX+CA+IL = 48%.

 State distribution (measured, all 13 territory states)

 TX 19,234 / CA 9,404 / IL 8,333 / PA 7,294 / OH 6,745 / MI 5,352 / MA 4,436 / IN 4,034 / OK 3,809 / TN 3,122 / CT 2,364 / NE 1,353 / NV 988. All in-territory — no out-of-territory bleed.

 Important caveat on math filter

 Only 3 rows (measured) got excluded — because the SF Leads tab is already the non-math subset. The SF Leads - Math , SF Contacts - Math , etc. tabs (visible in your screenshot) hold math-related rows separately.

 So the tab structure already does the filtering and our keyword filter was redundant. No action needed.

 What this means for Strategy #12 lead source

 Your actionable dormant pool isn't the 90-day dormant Scout-activity list — it's segments of these 76,468 SF leads. Likely candidates:

 - teacher-created-account empty-title (24,491 measured) — self-identified teachers who signed up and never bought. This IS the dormant teacher pool Scout has been missing.

 - Plus teacher with Title populated (3,416 measured) — explicit teachers from other sources

 - Admin pool (28,892) better suits a separate admin-variant sequence later

 That's ~27,907 (estimate) addressable dormant teacher contacts in SF Imports right now — no research engine needed, no CC backend blocker. At ~266/week (estimate, Tier 1 budget share for #12), that's 104 weeks

 of supply at steady state.

 Implications for sequence architecture

 - "Dormant" (#12) shouldn't be one generic sequence — it should be a family of 3-5 sequences sharing a "re-engage" stance but swapping:

 - Cohort source (teacher-created vs license request vs quote/demo vs library)

 - Role variant (admin / teacher / IT / curriculum)

 - That matches the C4 pattern (4 variants — Teachers / Admin / School General / District General) and the CUE 26' pattern (6 variants by grade level). Variants share angles but target different readers.

 Two filters Steven mentioned — worth codifying

 1. Active-account cross-reference filter:

 - If a lead's company maps to an active account where Deal Level = district → exclude the lead (CSMs handle, hands-off for outbound)

 - If a lead's company maps to an active account where Deal Level = school → lead is fair game IF their school isn't that specific active-school

 - If lead's company doesn't match any active account → fair game

 - Helper needed: filter_leads_against_active_accounts(leads) -> (allowed, excluded_district_match, excluded_school_match) . Uses csv_importer.get_active_accounts() (already exists) + your fuzzy-match helper.

 2. 90-day no-touch filter via Outreach activity:

 - Outreach API exposes per-prospect mailings/calls/events via get_mailings_for_prospect(prospect_id) (already in tools.outreach_client )

 - Filter rule: include prospect IF max(last_mailing_date, last_call_date, last_event_date) is >= 90 days ago, OR no activity at all

 - Helper needed: find_prospects_with_no_touch(days=90, sequence_id_exclude=None) -> list[prospect] — bulk-queries /events or /mailings with date filters. Reusable across all substrategies.

 Where should this analysis + sequence family live?

 1. The revised copy framework you're dialing in claude.ai (serves as the base voice/structure)

 2. The active-account filter helper

 3. The 90-day no-touch filter helper

 4. A cohort-extraction script that pulls segments from SF Imports filtered by Active Accounts

 Then each substrategy = campaigns/<slug>.md using the same loader pattern ( scripts/load_campaign.py ), variant-split by role, with cohort CSV pre-filtered.

 Does this match your mental model?

 If yes, the next-session plan becomes:

 1. Receive revised base copy from you (claude.ai) → anchor it as the Dormant voice framework

 2. Build filter_leads_against_active_accounts helper

 3. Build find_prospects_with_no_touch helper (Outreach activity lookup)

 4. Pull one specific substrategy cohort (e.g., "teacher-created empty-title + 60+ days no touch + not in active district") — report size

 5. You pick which substrategy to ship first, build variants, create sequences, you activate, load at Tier 1 budget rate (~266/week estimate)

 Grade applies only to School Admin and Teacher (where school-level role makes grade relevant). IT, Curriculum, and District Admin are typically district-wide, no grade split.

 Grade-level detection — feasible?

 Yes, via lightweight rules on Company field + Verified School column (the latter is populated in SF Imports per the screenshot):

 - elementary , elem , primary , K-5 , K-6 → Elem

 - middle , junior high , intermediate , 6-8 , MS → MS

 - high school , senior high , 9-12 , HS → HS

 - ambiguous (K-12 district-wide, academy, PK-12 private, unknown) → route to the "School General" catch-all OR skip grade variant

 Cost: $0 (measured) — keyword rules. Can also cross-ref tools.territory_data.Territory Schools tab which has Grade Span column (measured from screenshot) for enrichment.

 Sequence name format

 DRE-<SUBSTRATEGY>-<ROLE>-<GRADE> | <Descriptive Title>

 Examples:

 - DRE-TC-TCH-HS | Dormant Teacher-Created — High School Teachers

 - DRE-TC-ADM-DST | Dormant Teacher-Created — District Admin

 - DRE-LR-TCH-MS | License Request — Middle School Teachers

 - DRE-QD-IT | Quote-Demo — IT/Technology Leaders

 - DRE-LIB-LIB | Library Re-Engage — Librarians

 Prefix keys:

 - DRE = Dormant Re-Engage family (Strategy #12)

 - Substrategy: TC =Teacher-Created / LR =License Request / QD =Quote/Demo / LIB =Library

 - Role: ADM =Admin / TCH =Teacher / IT / CUR =Curriculum / LIB =Librarian

 - Grade: DST =District-level / ELM =Elementary / MS / HS / (blank if N/A)

 Tag convention (applied to prospects at add-time)

 - dre-2026-spring (campaign identifier — every DRE prospect)

 - dre-<substrategy> (e.g., dre-teacher-created , dre-license-request , dre-quote-demo , dre-library )

 - dre-role-<role> (e.g., dre-role-teacher , dre-role-admin-district , dre-role-admin-school , dre-role-it )

 - dre-grade-<grade> (only if grade-split: dre-grade-hs , dre-grade-ms , dre-grade-elem )

 - dre-state-<state> (e.g., dre-state-tx ) — already enforced by Rule 17 state population

 - dre-cohort-<source> (e.g., dre-cohort-sf-leads , dre-cohort-sf-contacts , dre-cohort-outreach-90d )

 Benefits of this convention

 - Filter by substrategy in Outreach UI: search DRE-TC to see all Teacher-Created variants

 - Cross-tag analysis: Steven can pull reply rates by grade, by role, by cohort source

 - Performance tuning: Week 3-4 data review per S66 plan — dial budget up on whatever tag is winning

 - Future-proof: When we extend to Strategies #16-20 (signal-driven), use parallel SIG- , BND- (bond triggers), HIRE- (new hire), GRANT- prefixes

 - dre-role-teacher , dre-role-admin-district , dre-role-admin-school , dre-role-it , dre-role-curriculum , dre-role-librarian , dre-role-unknown

 1. Substrategy A teacher-created — 24,491 pool (measured). Ship first. 9 variants.

 2. Substrategy B license/quote/demo — extends existing C4. Cohort size TBD from Lead Source filter. 9 variants.

 3. Substrategy D universal — catches whatever falls through role classification (empty-OS 2,513 measured + other 1,528 measured + unclassifiable slice = ~4,000+ estimate). 1 sequence. Ship early as safety net.

 4. Substrategy C library/librarian — 379 librarians (measured). Small, ship last.

 Note: D (Universal) might actually deserve to ship 2nd or even 1st so no cohort falls through the cracks — it's the safety net.

 40% pause protocol — per the system alert

 Handoff note is current at .claude/handoff.md . I'll update it with the revised 21-sequence framing (substrategy merge + universal add) as my final atomic op before you send EOS.

 Options for you:

 (a) Full wrap-up now — I'll update handoff, commit S71 artifacts, prepare final EOS summary

 (b) Send your EOS prompt — I'll treat it as the standard end-of-session routine

 (c) One more named item — tell me specifically what and I'll do only that

 ❯ We're approaching context limits. Before I hit /exit, do the following in order:

 1. Save any feedback, preferences, or user context learned this session to memory

 2. Update the project plan file (e.g. SCOUT_PLAN.md):

 - Mark completed items as done

 - Add any new tasks or additions that came up this session

 - Update 'Current Focus' to reflect exactly where we are

 - If we're mid-task, note precisely what was being attempted and what state it's in

 3. Maintain CLAUDE.md:

 - Add any new rules, conventions, or architectural decisions from this session

 - Update 'Current State' with: what's working, what's broken or in-progress, and the exact next step to take

 - Note any uncommitted or in-progress code changes that the next session needs to be aware of

 - Review for content that is no longer actively relevant to ongoing work (e.g. completed features, resolved decisions, old state). If found, move it IN FULL to the project history

 file — do not summarize or lose detail. If everything in CLAUDE.md is still relevant, don't trim anything.

 4. Update the project history file (e.g. SCOUT_HISTORY.md):

 - Append a numbered session entry (e.g. "## Session 14") with: what changed, why, key decisions, and anything archived from CLAUDE.md

 5. Commit and push all changes

 6. Read back CLAUDE.md and the plan file to verify everything is captured correctly. If anything is missing or inaccurate, fix it before proceeding. Then give me:

 - A brief summary of what we accomplished this session

 - The exact prompt to paste in the next session, which should tell Claude to read the plan file and continue from the Current State in CLAUDE.md

 12 + - **Thursday 2026-04-16 diocesan drip — done.** 14 contacts loaded live (measured), 63/63 total plans `done`. Latent bug discovered: Steven's desktop-app attempt at 11:08 CDT had failed silently

 + with `sequence_not_enabled` because `/tmp/outreach_tokens.json` was missing (tmp cleared on reboot). Fix: copied `memory/outreach_tokens.json` → `/tmp/...`, force-refreshed, verified all 6 seqs

 + enabled, reset 14 `skipped` → `pending`, re-executed. Tracked as `project_diocesan_drip_silent_skip_on_missing_tmp_token.md`.

 13 + - **C4 cold_license_request Queue status drift cleaned.** Starting state: 1,245 pending (measured). Steven's hunch "I'm 90% sure there's a mistake" validated: 1,092 rows (measured) were ALREADY

 + in C4 seqs 1995-1998 (S43 loaded them but Queue status was never back-written). Additional audit of the 150 "unmatched" via live Outreach: 7 newly loaded (measured), 86 already in OTHER sequence

 + s (measured), 46 opted-out (measured), 11 no-state (measured), 3 empty email. Result state: 1,099 complete / 143 skipped (w/ distinctive Notes markers for filtering) / 3 true pending. Scripts ra

 + n bulk sheet batchUpdate with 21,840 cells updated (measured) + 3,000 cells updated (measured) for classification cleanup.

 14 + - **24-strategy sequence coverage mapped accurately.** Prior CLAUDE.md framing "build sequences for the 23 strategies" was wrong — 18 of 24 have zero Scout-helped sequences. New authoritative me

 + mory: `project_sequence_coverage_s71.md`. 5 of 24 strategies + diocesan have Scout-helped sequences (#7, #9/#11 shared, #10, #13, #14). Steven confirmed line-by-line which seqs Scout helped vs h

 + e built solo.

 15 + - **Dormant Re-Engage Strategy #12 evolved to DRE family.** Not one sequence — **21 sequences** (4 substrategies × role × grade): TC (Teacher-Created) 9 variants, LQD (License/Quote/Demo — merge

 + d) 9 variants, LIB (Library/Librarian) 2 variants, UNI (Universal fallback) 1 variant. Full framework in `project_dre_family_framework.md`. Naming convention `DRE-<SUB>-<ROLE>-<GRADE>` + tag con

 + vention documented. Build order: UNI (safety net first), TC, LQD, LIB.

 16 + - **SF Leads role+state segmentation complete.** 76,468 leads in-territory (measured). Top buckets: admin 28,892 (measured — 37.78% measured), empty-TC 24,491 (measured — 32.03% measured), it 9,

 + 873 (measured). TX dominates at 25% of universe (measured). Full rollups in subagent output file; key insight: the empty-TC (teacher-created, empty title) cohort is THE dormant teacher pool for

 + Strategy #12 sub-A.

 + ). Steven explicitly flagged this as crucial after Scout couldn't answer "what's the sheet ID" on its own.

 + NI first as safety net.

 1583 ---
 1585 + ## Session 71 (2026-04-16) — Thursday Diocesan Drip Done + C4 Status Drift Cleaned + Sequence Coverage Mapped + DRE Family Framework Evolved
 1586 +
 1587 + **Session theme:** Thursday 2026-04-16 diocesan drip executed + major audit of Queue status drift (triggered by Steven's "90% sure there's a mistake" on the 1,245+247 backlog numbers) + 24-str
 + ategy sequence coverage mapped authoritatively + Strategy #12 Dormant Re-Engage framework evolved from one sequence into a 21-sequence family spanning 4 substrategies × role × grade. Dormant v

 + 2 draft shipped to Google Doc; Steven took to claude.ai for copy dial-in at EOS.

 1588 +

 1589 + ### 1. Thursday diocesan drip — landed live

 1590 +

 1591 + - `scripts/diocesan_drip.py --execute` completed for 2026-04-16 Thursday batch. 14 contacts loaded live (measured — prospects 669638-669652, sequenceStates 522473-522486). Distribution: Boston

 + 1 / Cincinnati 4 / Detroit 2 / Philadelphia 4 / Cleveland 3.

 1592 + - Latent bug discovered: Steven's desktop Claude app had attempted the drip at 11:08 CDT earlier today and failed silently. `/tmp/outreach_tokens.json` was missing (Mac rebooted, tmp cleared).

 + The sequence-enabled pre-check in `prospect_loader._sequence_is_enabled` fail-closed on the auth error, marking all 14 contacts as `skipped` with `error=sequence_not_enabled`. The 6 diocesan

 + sequences were actually enabled.

 1593 + - Fix applied: copied `memory/outreach_tokens.json` → `/tmp/outreach_tokens.json` to rehydrate the module-load path, force-refreshed via `_refresh_access_token()`, verified all 6 seqs `enabled

 + =True` via `_api_get('/sequences/{sid}')` (Rule 21 read-before-write satisfied), reset 14 `skipped` → `pending` with error cleared, re-executed. All 14 POSTed cleanly. `--verify` passed clean

 + for all 6 sequences.

 1594 + - Tracked as project memory: `project_diocesan_drip_silent_skip_on_missing_tmp_token.md`. Recommends a pre-flight smoke test in `cmd_execute` before the batch loop — one `_api_get('/sequences/

 + 1')` call that aborts loudly if auth is broken, replacing the current silent fail-closed behavior.

 1595 +

 1596 + ### 2. C4 cold_license_request Queue status drift cleanup — 1,235 rows reclassified

 1597 +

 1598 + - Steven's hunch: "I'm 90% sure there's a mistake you have made with the '1,245 cold_license_request + 247 winback March' but we can address that as we get there." Validated.

 1599 + - Starting state measured via `tools.district_prospector.get_all_prospects()`: 1,245 pending cold_license_request rows. Cross-referenced against C4 sequences 1995-1998 live sequenceStates (1,1

 + 19 total prospects, 1,137 unique emails measured): **1,092 of the 1,245 pending rows (measured — 87.7%) were ALREADY in C4 sequences.** The S43 load happened but the Queue status was never bac

 + k-written. Drift.

 1600 + - Backfill write #1: built `/tmp/c4_status_backfill.py` — read 3 columns, keyword-match emails vs C4 pool, single-pass batchUpdate. Updated 1,092 rows (measured) to Status=`complete` with Note

 + s suffix `Loaded into C4 seq <id> (<name>) — S71 2026-04-16`. 21,840 cells updated (measured), 138 contiguous ranges.

 1601 + - Attempted to load the 150 "unmatched" (i.e., not in C4 sequences) into C4 variants via `prospect_loader.execute_load_plan`. Results: 139 routed (11 skipped at plan time for missing State per

 + Rule 17). Live results: **7 processed** (measured — genuinely new adds), **86 failed with `active in another sequence`** (measured — already being outreached elsewhere, correct NOT to double-

 + add), **46 failed with `opted_out_of_email`** (measured).

 1602 + - Backfill write #2: built `/tmp/c4_backfill_cleanup.py` — classified all 150 by outcome and wrote Queue rows with distinctive Notes markers so future sessions can filter. Result state: 1,099

 + complete / 143 skipped w/ reasons / 3 true pending. 150 rows updated (measured), 3,000 cells updated (measured), 115 contiguous ranges.

 1603 + - Distinctive Notes markers (for Steven's later filtering):

 1604 + - `Loaded into C4 seq <id> (<name>) — S71 2026-04-16` → 1,099 complete

 1605 + - `ACTIVE IN OTHER OUTREACH SEQUENCE (not C4) — S71 2026-04-16 — review in Outreach if needed` → 86 skipped (Steven plans to review these later; distinct Notes marker avoids needing a custom

 + Outreach tag right now)

 1606 + - `Opted out of email per Outreach — S71 2026-04-16` → 46 skipped

 1607 + - `No State in Queue; Haiku enrichment returned UNKNOWN — S71 2026-04-16 — manual review if valuable` → 11 skipped

 1608 + - Steven's insight that reframed everything: the "1,245 cold_license_request pending" backlog was mostly drift (1,092) and conflict (86+46 not addressable). Actual net-new adds from that cohor

 + t: 7 (measured). The Queue's "898 remaining pending across other strategies" after the cleanup are all district-level stubs without emails — research-engine-dependent (parked) or CC-backend-de

 + pendent (parked). The only immediately sequenceable backlog was the 150 (now resolved).

 1609 +

 1610 + ### 3. 24-strategy sequence coverage mapped accurately — `project_sequence_coverage_s71.md`

 1611 +

 1612 + - Prior CLAUDE.md / S66 / S67 framing "build sequences for the 23 active prospecting strategies" was wrong. New authoritative map: **5 of 24 strategies + diocesan have Scout-helped sequences.

 + 18 have zero Scout-helped sequences.**

 1613 + - Scout-helped sequences (confirmed line-by-line with Steven):

 1614 + - #7 Role/Title: Superintendent CS/AI state variants (1959-1971, 13 sequences)

 1615 + - #9 Winback + #11 Unresponsive Re-Engage (shared): 5-Step Re-Engagement Seq 2026 cold ops (1982)

 1616 + - #10 Cold License Request: C4 1995-1998 + !!!2026 License Request Seq April (1999)

 1617 + - #13 Post-Conference: CUE 26' family (12 seqs: 1983-1994)

 1618 + - #14 Webinar: Algebra Webinar April 2026 Attendees (2000) + Non-attendees (2001) only; Hour of AI, Leveraging AI, AI x Algebra NOT Scout-helped

 1619 + - Diocesan (secondary lane, not on 24-list): 2008-2013 (6 seqs)

 1620 + - Steven confirmed via UI screenshots that Q1 D-Prosp Denison (1954-1956), Q1DPROSP Cheatham TN (1973-1975), 2026 Q1 Algebra AI Launch (1977/1979/1980), Algebra 30-day free (2005-2007), BETT/A

 + CTE/FETC (1938-1942), !!!2026 Opp Follow up (1976) were NOT Scout-helped — he did them solo or with claude.ai.

 1621 + - Steven also corrected: Superintendent CS/AI state sequences belong under #7 Role/Title-Based, NOT #4 Territory Touching. Territory Touching (#4) remains uncovered.

 1622 + - Gaps clustered: all signal-driven strategies (#16-20, 22) have Scanners feeding the Signals tab but zero sequences to deliver against those signals. That's the structural bottleneck blocking

 + signal-driven sales motion.

 1623 +

 1624 + ### 4. Dormant Re-Engage Strategy #12 evolved from one sequence to a 21-sequence DRE family

 1625 +

 1626 + - Started: build one Dormant Re-Engage sequence per S66 plan. Shipped v1 + v2 drafts to Google Docs.

 1627 + - v1 Google Doc: `https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Id SzLxJ5AJ90cQRNj1kxe9k Tz4wyw Pb7YFeE7Dkm WiU/edit`

 1628 + - v2 Google Doc: `https://docs.google.com/document/d/164JXmN0wZ7sz_d4_4SgPINJncsxumz5zwgzzhBLBLn0/edit` — meeting link `https://hello.codecombat.com/c/steven/t/131` inserted, info dump templat

 + e 43784 at Step 2, "Quick follow up" / "One more angle worth mentioning" removed, HTML anchor tags stripped for Google Doc readability (will be re-added as HTML when create_sequence ships to O

 + utreach).

 1629 + - Steven's feedback evolved the framework: (a) substrategies for Teacher-Created / License-Quote-Demo / Library / Universal, (b) role variants (District Admin / School Admin / Teacher / IT / C

 + urriculum / Librarian), (c) grade variants where applicable (HS / MS / Elem), (d) active-account cross-ref filter, (e) 90-day no-touch Outreach activity filter.

 1630 + - Merged License Request + Quote/Demo into one substrategy per Steven: "License request and Quote/Demo Request are the exact same thing."

 1631 + - Added Universal fallback per Steven: "We should also have a generic universal sequence for the ones where we don't have or can't find the title/role data."

 1632 + - Final DRE family: **21 sequences** = TC(9) + LQD(9) + LIB(2) + UNI(1). Full framework + naming + tag conventions documented in `project_dre_family_framework.md`.

 1633 + - Build order decided: UNI first as safety net, then TC (biggest cohort at 24,491 measured empty-TC + 3,416 measured explicit teachers), then LQD, then LIB (379 measured librarians).

 1634 +

 1635 + ### 5. SF Leads segmentation — 76,468 in-territory leads analyzed ($0 measured)

 1636 +

 1637 + - Discovered during session: SF Imports sheet ID (`15p Smpfd SlgoaBFxbwqu UjtO9xYSnK-4yA69mkw_lWLk`) was NOT in `.env`. Steven flagged this as crucial: "THIS IS CRUCIAL. You need to make sure you

 + have access to all this data." Now documented in `reference_scout_sheet_ids.md`.

 1638 + - Subagent dispatched to produce role + state + cross-tab rollups from the `SF Leads` tab. Keyword-based classification only, no Haiku calls. Cost $0 (measured). Script preserved at `/tmp/sf_l

 + eads_segmentation.py`.

 1639 + - Role distribution (measured, n=76,468): admin 28,892 (37.78%) / empty-TC 24,491 (32.03%) / it 9,873 (12.91%) / curriculum 4,252 (5.56%) / teacher 3,416 (4.47%) / empty-OS 2,513 (3.29%) / oth

 + er 1,528 (2.00%) / admin_support 1,106 (1.45%) / library 379 (0.50%) / counselor 17 / coach 1.

 1640 + - State distribution (measured, all 13 territory states + SoCal): TX 19,234 / CA 9,404 / IL 8,333 / PA 7,294 / OH 6,745 / MI 5,352 / MA 4,436 / IN 4,034 / OK 3,809 / TN 3,122 / CT 2,364 / NE 1

 + ,353 / NV 988. TX is 25% of universe.

 1641 + - Noteworthy: the algebra/math filter only hit 3 rows (measured) because the Scout SF Imports workbook already separates math into dedicated `SF Leads - Math` / `SF Contacts - Math` tabs. The

 + main `SF Leads` tab is already non-math.

 1642 + - Also noteworthy: 24,491 empty-title rows where Lead Source = "teacher created account" IS the dormant teacher pool Scout has been missing. Plus 3,416 explicit teachers = ~27,907 addressable

 + dormant teacher contacts in SF Imports (no research engine needed, no CC backend blocker).

 1643 +

 1644 + ### 6. New memory files shipped

 1645 +

 1646 + - `reference_scout_sheet_ids.md` — authoritative sheet ID map

 1647 + - `project_sequence_coverage_s71.md` — 24-strategy coverage authoritative

 1648 + - `project_dre_family_framework.md` — DRE family 21-sequence framework

 1649 + - `project_diocesan_drip_silent_skip_on_missing_tmp_token.md` — latent bug tracked

 1650 + - `feedback_chart_format_preference.md` — chart-over-text-list preference

 1651 +

 1652 + ### 7. Uncommitted work passed to next session

 1653 +

 1654 + - Dormant Re-Engage sequence not yet created in Outreach — Steven is dial-in copy in claude.ai; next session receives revised copy, then builds DRE family starting with UNI safety-net.

 1655 + - `GOOGLE_SHEETS_SF_IMPORTS_ID=15p Smpfd SlgoaBFxbwqu UjtO9xYSnK-4yA69mkw_lWLk` should be added to `.env` next session.

 1656 + - The 3 DRE-family filter helpers need to be built: `filter_leads_against_active_accounts`, `find_prospects_with_no_touch(days=90)`, grade-level detector.

 Implications for sequence architecture

 - "Dormant" (#12) shouldn't be one generic sequence — it should be a family of 3-5 sequences sharing a "re-engage" stance but swapping:

 - Cohort source (teacher-created vs license request vs quote/demo vs library)

 - Role variant (admin / teacher / IT / curriculum)

 - That matches the C4 pattern (4 variants — Teachers / Admin / School General / District General) and the CUE 26' pattern (6 variants by grade level). Variants share angles but target different readers.

 Two filters Steven mentioned — worth codifying

 1. Active-account cross-reference filter:

 - If a lead's company maps to an active account where Deal Level = district → exclude the lead (CSMs handle, hands-off for outbound)

 - If a lead's company maps to an active account where Deal Level = school → lead is fair game IF their school isn't that specific active-school

 - If lead's company doesn't match any active account → fair game

 - Helper needed: filter_leads_against_active_accounts(leads) -> (allowed, excluded_district_match, excluded_school_match) . Uses csv_importer.get_active_accounts() (already exists) + your fuzzy-match helper.

 2. 90-day no-touch filter via Outreach activity:

 - Outreach API exposes per-prospect mailings/calls/events via get_mailings_for_prospect(prospect_id) (already in tools.outreach_client )

 - Filter rule: include prospect IF max(last_mailing_date, last_call_date, last_event_date) is >= 90 days ago, OR no activity at all

 - Helper needed: find_prospects_with_no_touch(days=90, sequence_id_exclude=None) -> list[prospect] — bulk-queries /events or /mailings with date filters. Reusable across all substrategies.

 Where should this analysis + sequence family live?

 1. The revised copy framework you're dialing in claude.ai (serves as the base voice/structure)

 2. The active-account filter helper

 3. The 90-day no-touch filter helper

 4. A cohort-extraction script that pulls segments from SF Imports filtered by Active Accounts

 Then each substrategy = campaigns/<slug>.md using the same loader pattern ( scripts/load_campaign.py ), variant-split by role, with cohort CSV pre-filtered.

 Does this match your mental model?

 If yes, the next-session plan becomes:

 1. Receive revised base copy from you (claude.ai) → anchor it as the Dormant voice framework

 2. Build filter_leads_against_active_accounts helper

 3. Build find_prospects_with_no_touch helper (Outreach activity lookup)

 4. Pull one specific substrategy cohort (e.g., "teacher-created empty-title + 60+ days no touch + not in active district") — report size

 5. You pick which substrategy to ship first, build variants, create sequences, you activate, load at Tier 1 budget rate (~266/week estimate)

 Grade applies only to School Admin and Teacher (where school-level role makes grade relevant). IT, Curriculum, and District Admin are typically district-wide, no grade split.

 Grade-level detection — feasible?

 Yes, via lightweight rules on Company field + Verified School column (the latter is populated in SF Imports per the screenshot):

 - elementary , elem , primary , K-5 , K-6 → Elem

 - middle , junior high , intermediate , 6-8 , MS → MS

 - high school , senior high , 9-12 , HS → HS

 - ambiguous (K-12 district-wide, academy, PK-12 private, unknown) → route to the "School General" catch-all OR skip grade variant

 Cost: $0 (measured) — keyword rules. Can also cross-ref tools.territory_data.Territory Schools tab which has Grade Span column (measured from screenshot) for enrichment.

 Sequence name format

 DRE-<SUBSTRATEGY>-<ROLE>-<GRADE> | <Descriptive Title>

 Examples:

 - DRE-TC-TCH-HS | Dormant Teacher-Created — High School Teachers

 - DRE-TC-ADM-DST | Dormant Teacher-Created — District Admin

 - DRE-LR-TCH-MS | License Request — Middle School Teachers

 - DRE-QD-IT | Quote-Demo — IT/Technology Leaders

 - DRE-LIB-LIB | Library Re-Engage — Librarians

 Prefix keys:

 - DRE = Dormant Re-Engage family (Strategy #12)

 - Substrategy: TC =Teacher-Created / LR =License Request / QD =Quote/Demo / LIB =Library

 - Role: ADM =Admin / TCH =Teacher / IT / CUR =Curriculum / LIB =Librarian

 - Grade: DST =District-level / ELM =Elementary / MS / HS / (blank if N/A)

 Tag convention (applied to prospects at add-time)

 - dre-2026-spring (campaign identifier — every DRE prospect)

 - dre-<substrategy> (e.g., dre-teacher-created , dre-license-request , dre-quote-demo , dre-library )

 - dre-role-<role> (e.g., dre-role-teacher , dre-role-admin-district , dre-role-admin-school , dre-role-it )

 - dre-grade-<grade> (only if grade-split: dre-grade-hs , dre-grade-ms , dre-grade-elem )

 - dre-state-<state> (e.g., dre-state-tx ) — already enforced by Rule 17 state population

 - dre-cohort-<source> (e.g., dre-cohort-sf-leads , dre-cohort-sf-contacts , dre-cohort-outreach-90d )

 Benefits of this convention

 - Filter by substrategy in Outreach UI: search DRE-TC to see all Teacher-Created variants

 - Cross-tag analysis: Steven can pull reply rates by grade, by role, by cohort source

 - Performance tuning: Week 3-4 data review per S66 plan — dial budget up on whatever tag is winning

 - Future-proof: When we extend to Strategies #16-20 (signal-driven), use parallel SIG- , BND- (bond triggers), HIRE- (new hire), GRANT- prefixes

 + 18 have zero Scout-helped sequences.**

 1613 + - Scout-helped sequences (confirmed line-by-line with Steven):

 1614 + - #7 Role/Title: Superintendent CS/AI state variants (1959-1971, 13 sequences)

 1615 + - #9 Winback + #11 Unresponsive Re-Engage (shared): 5-Step Re-Engagement Seq 2026 cold ops (1982)

 1617 + - #13 Post-Conference: CUE 26' family (12 seqs: 1983-1994)

 + CTE/FETC (1938-1942), !!!2026 Opp Follow up (1976) were NOT Scout-helped — he did them solo or with claude.ai.

 + utreach).

 40 + ## Why (session-governing context)

 31 - ### CRIT-2 — `gas/Code.gs:30` (doPost entry) — placeholder token guard

 32 - Added a runtime check at the top of `doPost` that returns HTTP-500 `"Server misconfigured: SECRET_TOKEN not set"` if `SECRET_TOKEN === "REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_SECRET_TOKEN_HERE"` or empty. Prevents a

 - new deploy from silently accepting any caller who sends the placeholder string.

 42 + - S71 was supposed to start with the Thursday diocesan drip (scheduled S66/S70) and then kick off Tier 1 sequence build (S67 focus). Both happened. Bonus: Steven's "are these numbers right?" hun

 Only 3 rows (measured) got excluded — because the SF Leads tab is already the non-math subset. The SF Leads - Math , SF Contacts - Math , etc. tabs (visible in your screenshot) hold math-related rows separately.

 Implications for sequence architecture

 - "Dormant" (#12) shouldn't be one generic sequence — it should be a family of 3-5 sequences sharing a "re-engage" stance but swapping:

 - Cohort source (teacher-created vs license request vs quote/demo vs library)

 - Role variant (admin / teacher / IT / curriculum)

 - That matches the C4 pattern (4 variants — Teachers / Admin / School General / District General) and the CUE 26' pattern (6 variants by grade level). Variants share angles but target different readers.

 Two filters Steven mentioned — worth codifying

 1. Active-account cross-reference filter:

 - If a lead's company maps to an active account where Deal Level = district → exclude the lead (CSMs handle, hands-off for outbound)

 - If a lead's company maps to an active account where Deal Level = school → lead is fair game IF their school isn't that specific active-school

 - If lead's company doesn't match any active account → fair game

 - Helper needed: filter_leads_against_active_accounts(leads) -> (allowed, excluded_district_match, excluded_school_match) . Uses csv_importer.get_active_accounts() (already exists) + your fuzzy-match helper.

 2. 90-day no-touch filter via Outreach activity:

 - Outreach API exposes per-prospect mailings/calls/events via get_mailings_for_prospect(prospect_id) (already in tools.outreach_client )

 - Filter rule: include prospect IF max(last_mailing_date, last_call_date, last_event_date) is >= 90 days ago, OR no activity at all

 - Helper needed: find_prospects_with_no_touch(days=90, sequence_id_exclude=None) -> list[prospect] — bulk-queries /events or /mailings with date filters. Reusable across all substrategies.

 Where should this analysis + sequence family live?

 1. The revised copy framework you're dialing in claude.ai (serves as the base voice/structure)

 2. The active-account filter helper

 3. The 90-day no-touch filter helper

 4. A cohort-extraction script that pulls segments from SF Imports filtered by Active Accounts

 Then each substrategy = campaigns/<slug>.md using the same loader pattern ( scripts/load_campaign.py ), variant-split by role, with cohort CSV pre-filtered.

 Does this match your mental model?

 If yes, the next-session plan becomes:

 1. Receive revised base copy from you (claude.ai) → anchor it as the Dormant voice framework

 2. Build filter_leads_against_active_accounts helper

 3. Build find_prospects_with_no_touch helper (Outreach activity lookup)

 4. Pull one specific substrategy cohort (e.g., "teacher-created empty-title + 60+ days no touch + not in active district") — report size

 5. You pick which substrategy to ship first, build variants, create sequences, you activate, load at Tier 1 budget rate (~266/week estimate)

 Grade applies only to School Admin and Teacher (where school-level role makes grade relevant). IT, Curriculum, and District Admin are typically district-wide, no grade split.

 Grade-level detection — feasible?

 Yes, via lightweight rules on Company field + Verified School column (the latter is populated in SF Imports per the screenshot):

 - elementary , elem , primary , K-5 , K-6 → Elem

 - middle , junior high , intermediate , 6-8 , MS → MS

 - high school , senior high , 9-12 , HS → HS

 - ambiguous (K-12 district-wide, academy, PK-12 private, unknown) → route to the "School General" catch-all OR skip grade variant

 Cost: $0 (measured) — keyword rules. Can also cross-ref tools.territory_data.Territory Schools tab which has Grade Span column (measured from screenshot) for enrichment.

 Sequence name format

 DRE-<SUBSTRATEGY>-<ROLE>-<GRADE> | <Descriptive Title>

 Examples:

 - DRE-TC-TCH-HS | Dormant Teacher-Created — High School Teachers

 - DRE-TC-ADM-DST | Dormant Teacher-Created — District Admin

 - DRE-LR-TCH-MS | License Request — Middle School Teachers

 - DRE-QD-IT | Quote-Demo — IT/Technology Leaders

 - DRE-LIB-LIB | Library Re-Engage — Librarians

 Prefix keys:

 - DRE = Dormant Re-Engage family (Strategy #12)

 - Substrategy: TC =Teacher-Created / LR =License Request / QD =Quote/Demo / LIB =Library

 - Role: ADM =Admin / TCH =Teacher / IT / CUR =Curriculum / LIB =Librarian

 - Grade: DST =District-level / ELM =Elementary / MS / HS / (blank if N/A)

 Tag convention (applied to prospects at add-time)

 - dre-2026-spring (campaign identifier — every DRE prospect)

 - dre-<substrategy> (e.g., dre-teacher-created , dre-license-request , dre-quote-demo , dre-library )

 - dre-role-<role> (e.g., dre-role-teacher , dre-role-admin-district , dre-role-admin-school , dre-role-it )

 - dre-grade-<grade> (only if grade-split: dre-grade-hs , dre-grade-ms , dre-grade-elem )

 - dre-state-<state> (e.g., dre-state-tx ) — already enforced by Rule 17 state population

 - dre-cohort-<source> (e.g., dre-cohort-sf-leads , dre-cohort-sf-contacts , dre-cohort-outreach-90d )

 Benefits of this convention

 - Filter by substrategy in Outreach UI: search DRE-TC to see all Teacher-Created variants

 - Cross-tag analysis: Steven can pull reply rates by grade, by role, by cohort source

 - Performance tuning: Week 3-4 data review per S66 plan — dial budget up on whatever tag is winning

 - Future-proof: When we extend to Strategies #16-20 (signal-driven), use parallel SIG- , BND- (bond triggers), HIRE- (new hire), GRANT- prefixes

 │ B. License/Quote/Demo Re-Engage │ LQD │ Dist-Admin, School-Admin×3grades, Teacher×3grades, IT, Curr │ 9 │

 ├─────────────────────────────────┼──────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┤

 │ C. Library / Librarian │ LIB │ Dist-Admin (library), Librarian │ 2 │

 ├─────────────────────────────────┼──────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┤

 │ D. Universal Fallback (no role) │ UNI │ single generic │ 1 │

 ├─────────────────────────────────┼──────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┤

 │ Family total │ — │ — │ 21 sequences │

 └─────────────────────────────────┴──────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────┘

 Down from 29 (measured — prior draft had B+C separate and no universal). Merging B+C saved 9, adding universal added 1.

 Sequence name format: DRE-<SUBSTRATEGY>-<ROLE>-<GRADE> | <Descriptive Title>

 Examples:

 - DRE-TC-TCH-HS | Dormant Teacher-Created — High School Teachers

 - DRE-TC-ADM-DST | Dormant Teacher-Created — District Admin

 - DRE-LQD-TCH-MS | License/Quote/Demo — Middle School Teachers

 - DRE-LQD-IT | License/Quote/Demo — IT/Technology Leaders

 - DRE-LIB-LIB | Library Re-Engage — Librarians

 - DRE-UNI | Universal Fallback — No Role Identified

 Substrategy codes: TC / LQD / LIB / UNI

 Tag convention unchanged from prior turn — just swap license-request and quote-demo tags for single lqd tag:

 - dre-2026-spring

 - dre-tc / dre-lqd / dre-lib / dre-uni

 - dre-role-teacher , dre-role-admin-district , dre-role-admin-school , dre-role-it , dre-role-curriculum , dre-role-librarian , dre-role-unknown

 - dre-grade-hs / dre-grade-ms / dre-grade-elem (only if grade-split)

 - dre-state-<st>

 - dre-cohort-<src>

 1. Substrategy A teacher-created — 24,491 pool (measured). Ship first. 9 variants.

 2. Substrategy B license/quote/demo — extends existing C4. Cohort size TBD from Lead Source filter. 9 variants.

 3. Substrategy D universal — catches whatever falls through role classification (empty-OS 2,513 measured + other 1,528 measured + unclassifiable slice = ~4,000+ estimate). 1 sequence. Ship early as safety net.

 4. Substrategy C library/librarian — 379 librarians (measured). Small, ship last.

 Note: D (Universal) might actually deserve to ship 2nd or even 1st so no cohort falls through the cracks — it's the safety net.

 - 76,468 leads in-territory (3 filtered out as algebra/math noise — see caveat below)

 - 2nd biggest: teacher-created-account with empty title at 24,491 (32.03%) — your "assume teacher" pool

 - IT at 9,873 (12.91%), curriculum at 4,252 (5.56%), explicit teacher at 3,416 (4.47%)

 - TX = 25% of universe (19,234). TX+CA+IL = 48%.

 State distribution (measured, all 13 territory states)

 TX 19,234 / CA 9,404 / IL 8,333 / PA 7,294 / OH 6,745 / MI 5,352 / MA 4,436 / IN 4,034 / OK 3,809 / TN 3,122 / CT 2,364 / NE 1,353 / NV 988. All in-territory — no out-of-territory bleed.

 Important caveat on math filter

 Only 3 rows (measured) got excluded — because the SF Leads tab is already the non-math subset. The SF Leads - Math , SF Contacts - Math , etc. tabs (visible in your screenshot) hold math-related rows separately.

 So the tab structure already does the filtering and our keyword filter was redundant. No action needed.

 What this means for Strategy #12 lead source

 Your actionable dormant pool isn't the 90-day dormant Scout-activity list — it's segments of these 76,468 SF leads. Likely candidates:

 - teacher-created-account empty-title (24,491 measured) — self-identified teachers who signed up and never bought. This IS the dormant teacher pool Scout has been missing.

 - Plus teacher with Title populated (3,416 measured) — explicit teachers from other sources

 - Admin pool (28,892) better suits a separate admin-variant sequence later

 That's ~27,907 (estimate) addressable dormant teacher contacts in SF Imports right now — no research engine needed, no CC backend blocker. At ~266/week (estimate, Tier 1 budget share for #12), that's 104 weeks

 of supply at steady state.

 without spending anything. If you want that polish I can add those keyword rules next session — cost $0 (measured).

 Implications for sequence architecture

 - "Dormant" (#12) shouldn't be one generic sequence — it should be a family of 3-5 sequences sharing a "re-engage" stance but swapping:

 - Cohort source (teacher-created vs license request vs quote/demo vs library)

 - Role variant (admin / teacher / IT / curriculum)

 - That matches the C4 pattern (4 variants — Teachers / Admin / School General / District General) and the CUE 26' pattern (6 variants by grade level). Variants share angles but target different readers.

 Two filters Steven mentioned — worth codifying

 1. Active-account cross-reference filter:

 - If a lead's company maps to an active account where Deal Level = district → exclude the lead (CSMs handle, hands-off for outbound)

 - If a lead's company maps to an active account where Deal Level = school → lead is fair game IF their school isn't that specific active-school

 - If lead's company doesn't match any active account → fair game

 - Helper needed: filter_leads_against_active_accounts(leads) -> (allowed, excluded_district_match, excluded_school_match) . Uses csv_importer.get_active_accounts() (already exists) + your fuzzy-match helper.

 2. 90-day no-touch filter via Outreach activity:

 - Outreach API exposes per-prospect mailings/calls/events via get_mailings_for_prospect(prospect_id) (already in tools.outreach_client )

 - Filter rule: include prospect IF max(last_mailing_date, last_call_date, last_event_date) is >= 90 days ago, OR no activity at all

 - Helper needed: find_prospects_with_no_touch(days=90, sequence_id_exclude=None) -> list[prospect] — bulk-queries /events or /mailings with date filters. Reusable across all substrategies.

 Where should this analysis + sequence family live?

 1. The revised copy framework you're dialing in claude.ai (serves as the base voice/structure)

 2. The active-account filter helper

 3. The 90-day no-touch filter helper

 4. A cohort-extraction script that pulls segments from SF Imports filtered by Active Accounts

 Then each substrategy = campaigns/<slug>.md using the same loader pattern ( scripts/load_campaign.py ), variant-split by role, with cohort CSV pre-filtered.

 Does this match your mental model?

 If yes, the next-session plan becomes:

 1. Receive revised base copy from you (claude.ai) → anchor it as the Dormant voice framework

 2. Build filter_leads_against_active_accounts helper

 3. Build find_prospects_with_no_touch helper (Outreach activity lookup)

 4. Pull one specific substrategy cohort (e.g., "teacher-created empty-title + 60+ days no touch + not in active district") — report size

 5. You pick which substrategy to ship first, build variants, create sequences, you activate, load at Tier 1 budget rate (~266/week estimate)

 Grade applies only to School Admin and Teacher (where school-level role makes grade relevant). IT, Curriculum, and District Admin are typically district-wide, no grade split.

 Grade-level detection — feasible?

 Yes, via lightweight rules on Company field + Verified School column (the latter is populated in SF Imports per the screenshot):

 - elementary , elem , primary , K-5 , K-6 → Elem

 - middle , junior high , intermediate , 6-8 , MS → MS

 - high school , senior high , 9-12 , HS → HS

 - ambiguous (K-12 district-wide, academy, PK-12 private, unknown) → route to the "School General" catch-all OR skip grade variant

 Cost: $0 (measured) — keyword rules. Can also cross-ref tools.territory_data.Territory Schools tab which has Grade Span column (measured from screenshot) for enrichment.

 Sequence name format

 DRE-<SUBSTRATEGY>-<ROLE>-<GRADE> | <Descriptive Title>

 Examples:

 - DRE-TC-TCH-HS | Dormant Teacher-Created — High School Teachers

 - DRE-TC-ADM-DST | Dormant Teacher-Created — District Admin

 - DRE-LR-TCH-MS | License Request — Middle School Teachers

 - DRE-QD-IT | Quote-Demo — IT/Technology Leaders

 - DRE-LIB-LIB | Library Re-Engage — Librarians

 Prefix keys:

 - DRE = Dormant Re-Engage family (Strategy #12)

 - Substrategy: TC =Teacher-Created / LR =License Request / QD =Quote/Demo / LIB =Library

 - Role: ADM =Admin / TCH =Teacher / IT / CUR =Curriculum / LIB =Librarian

 - Grade: DST =District-level / ELM =Elementary / MS / HS / (blank if N/A)

 Tag convention (applied to prospects at add-time)

 - dre-2026-spring (campaign identifier — every DRE prospect)

 - dre-<substrategy> (e.g., dre-teacher-created , dre-license-request , dre-quote-demo , dre-library )

 - dre-role-<role> (e.g., dre-role-teacher , dre-role-admin-district , dre-role-admin-school , dre-role-it )

 - dre-grade-<grade> (only if grade-split: dre-grade-hs , dre-grade-ms , dre-grade-elem )

 - dre-state-<state> (e.g., dre-state-tx ) — already enforced by Rule 17 state population

 - dre-cohort-<source> (e.g., dre-cohort-sf-leads , dre-cohort-sf-contacts , dre-cohort-outreach-90d )

 Benefits of this convention

 - Filter by substrategy in Outreach UI: search DRE-TC to see all Teacher-Created variants

 - Cross-tag analysis: Steven can pull reply rates by grade, by role, by cohort source

 - Performance tuning: Week 3-4 data review per S66 plan — dial budget up on whatever tag is winning

 - Future-proof: When we extend to Strategies #16-20 (signal-driven), use parallel SIG- , BND- (bond triggers), HIRE- (new hire), GRANT- prefixes
